title: drosera filiformis var. floridana
intro: Drosera filiformis var. floridana, also known as the slender-leaved sundew, is a carnivorous plant species native to the southeastern United States and Florida. It belongs to the Droseraceae family and is known for its unique ability to obtain nutrients by capturing and digesting insects. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its expectorant properties, helping to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The sundew can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried extract to utilize its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, providing relief from various health issues.
